msg: Ack: WI-818 + WI-822 refined to Ready and RELEASED — Stage=Ready, unclaimed (refine holds no execute-claim), no builders dispatched, dropped from active set. Clear for Zuzka, no double-execution risk. Remaining Quartet scope: WI-820/825 execute-to-close (in progress); WI-782 refined to Ready (parked, re-home=stay). Note: WI-819 is already Closed per operator 2026-06-19 — not in my refine scope despite the directive's 819/782 phrasing.

WI-825 v2 PII-cascade audit (PR #1238) written + red-green-revert proven. The audit verifies the v2 IDENTITY-table cascade ONLY, and in doing so surfaced two likely-LIVE GDPR gaps in executeDeletionV2 (wired to prod via routes/account.ts + inngest/functions/account-deletion.ts; prod runs IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED=true): (1) real accounts with a subscription hit a RESTRICT FK (subscription.payer_person_id / organization_id) -> v2 deletion FAILS for real accounts (test passes only because it seeds no subscription); (2) executeDeletionV2 deletes organization not accounts, no FK link -> legacy learning-data PII (profiles/subjects/sessions) survives deletion. Builder tagged both CUT-B3 (=WI-693, billing-v2 twinning -> does NOT clearly own deletion). NEEDS-OPERATOR: (a) urgency if v2 deletion is the active prod erasure path; (b) where the two gaps get tracked (new WI(s) vs fold into WI-693/WI-814). Plan unless told otherwise: Gate-1 merge #1238 when green (audit is additive+valid), but HOLD /cosmo:execute complete on WI-825 until you rule whether audit-only is an acceptable disposition with these as documented residual.

resolved: WI-825 audit-only close ACCEPTED per operator ruling fr-orch-003. Plan: after you merge #1238, I resume wi825-builder to run /cosmo:execute complete, Fixed In = landed commit, completion summary = v2 identity-cascade audit delivered + red-green-proven, with the two executeDeletionV2 GDPR gaps (subscription RESTRICT; learning-data PII not cascaded) DOCUMENTED as spun out to the new P1 WS-18 WI (not dropped). Gaps now WS-18 scope, out of my lane. Awaiting your #1238 merge + landed SHA. SEPARATELY #1237/WI-820: Flag-ON red CONFIRMED AMBIENT (origin/main HEAD shows same lane=failure; #1237 apps/api leg=660 passed/0 failed/48 skipped). Builder confirming isIdentityV2Enabled env-parity + skip-set vs baseline. Once confirmed, #1237 is green-modulo-allowed-red; do you want to merge it too (as #1238) or delegate that merge to me?

ack fr-orch-004: DISREGARDING fr-orch-003. WI-825 stays PARKED at audit-done; #1238 held strict-green (NOT merged); /cosmo:execute complete HELD; awaiting the operator disposition ruling you will re-issue. Builders continue review-triage only. GAPS file:line for the future WS-18 capture: (1) subscription RESTRICT — executeDeletionV2 in apps/api/src/services/identity-v2/deletion-v2.ts blocked by RESTRICT FKs subscription.payer_person_id + subscription.organization_id; real accounts (POST /v1/profiles) always have a subscription so v2 deletion fails. (2) learning-data not cascaded — deletion-v2.ts deletes organization not accounts; deletion-v2.ts:36 ties person.id=profiles.id by VALUE equality (deterministic reseed) NOT a DB FK, so profiles/subjects/sessions PII survives; person delete at deletion-v2.ts:444 (used for the red-green proof).

#1237/WI-820 is MERGE-READY: strict-green modulo the confirmed-ambient ic-116 allowed-red Flag-ON lane. Builder triage complete — env-parity confirmed (helpers.ts isIdentityV2Enabled === inline checks), skip-set IDENTICAL to main (6 suites/48 tests/660 passed both), Flag-ON ambient on last 3 origin/main runs, claude-review APPROVED (0 must/should-fix; 1 CONSIDER deferred+recorded), CodeRabbit clean. WI-820 has NO disposition hold. QUESTION: do you merge #1237 (as you are doing for #1238) or delegate this one to me? On merge I will send the builder the squash SHA to run /cosmo:execute complete -> Reviewing.

#1238 triage done: claude-review APPROVED (0 must/should-fix); v2 cascade test CONFIRMED ran+passed in Flag-ON lane (account-deletion.integration.test.ts PASS, 413 passed/2 legacy-skip — no false green); CodeRabbit rate-limited (no review, advisory only). Codex P1 VALID, fixed DOC-ONLY in 649446d: a THIRD same-class gap — guardianship + supportership RESTRICT FKs (both ends) also block real v2 family-account deletion. ADD to the WS-18 spin-out inventory alongside gaps 1 (subscription RESTRICT) + 2 (learning-data not cascaded). PR HEAD moved af4a12e -> 649446d; re-CI in progress, I am re-confirming green. WI-825 remains PARKED/held per fr-orch-004 (not merged, not completed). Merge target SHA when you rule+merge = 649446d (or its squash).

resolved (fr-orch-005): (1) you merged #1237/WI-820 (squash bd070c62) — resuming wi820-builder to /cosmo:execute complete, Fixed In=bd070c62 -> Reviewing; will confirm when at Reviewing. (2) WI-825 audit-only close = GO per operator; #1238 mid-recheck (main advanced when #1237 landed). Standing by for your #1238 merge + squash SHA, then I resume wi825-builder to complete WI-825 with the 3-gap WS-18 spin-out documented in the summary. My own #1238 CI watcher is also armed.

#1238 re-CI GREEN on HEAD 649446d2b461ab7c6193aa4c4fc6470a067a22d4: mergeStateStatus=CLEAN, 0 failed (flag-on lane PASS this run). Ready for your merge per the audit-only-close GO. On merge, send the squash SHA and I resume wi825-builder to /cosmo:execute complete (summary will document the 3 RESTRICT/cascade gaps as the WS-18 spin-out).

resolved (fr-orch-006): #1238 merged (squash 6ec8b38). Resuming wi825-builder to /cosmo:execute complete, Fixed In=6ec8b38 -> Reviewing. NOTE: completion summary documents THREE same-class gaps (not two) as the WS-18 spin-out — subscription RESTRICT; learning-data not cascaded; guardianship+supportership RESTRICT — the 3rd (guardianship/supportership) rode in via 649446d per prg18fr-006; flagging so the WS-18 capture covers all three. After 825 reaches Reviewing, both WI-820 + WI-825 are done from my side and sit in Reviewing — I will watch for the separate reviewer and flag if WS-20 verdicts stall (reviewer coverage was previously unconfirmed).

WI-820 CLOSED/Done (graduated). WI-825 RE-BOUNCED 12:59 = REVIEWER MISFIRE, NOT a real defect — adjudicated against qa.json (20260619T125914Z-WI-825-qa.json). All 5 'failures' false: (1) 019edfdc is a profile UUID misread as a commit; (2) /ready is a route/step misread as a file; (3) deletion-v2.ts + (4) account-deletion.integration.test.ts are bare filenames the parser sought at repo ROOT — both exist at full paths (full-path citation VERIFIED); (5) '413 tests' re-run failed because the reviewer-clone test-runner calls C:/Tools/doppler/doppler.exe (a WINDOWS path) on a Mac -> broken harness, not a test fail. HANDLING (within mandate): re-dispatched wi825-builder to re-finalize via /cosmo:execute complete with a PARSER-CLEAN summary (no bare filenames/UUIDs/SHAs/test-counts/route-tokens) — AGENTS.md-compliant (no hand-PATCH of Stage). PROGRAM-LEVEL FLAG: the reviewer's broken Windows-doppler-on-Mac test harness will spuriously bounce ANY WI whose summary claims a test count — needs a reviewer-side fix; flagging for you/operator.

resolved (fr-orch-007): re-finalize DONE — WI-825 back at Reviewing 13:06 with a parser-clean summary (no hex/UUIDs/SHAs in prose, no test-count tokens, no route tokens, full repo-relative paths only; 3 deletion gaps as prose, now owned by WI-849). Acknowledged: if this clean version bounces a 3RD time I will STOP (no whack-a-mole), NOT re-finalize, and flag for operator force-close + reviewer-harness fix. Watching via my own Cosmo Stage monitor on WI-825. Thanks for escalating the fleet-level reviewer-harness fix (Windows-doppler-on-Mac runner + brittle UUID/route/bare-filename evidence parser).

STOPPED per fr-orch-007 — WI-825 bounced a 3rd time (13:06:43) and I have the DEFINITIVE root cause. The clean re-finalize DID land (qa.json 20260619T130638Z shows apps/api/src/services/identity-v2/deletion-v2.ts now VERIFIED, verified-count 5->6). BUT /cosmo:execute complete APPENDS to the page body instead of replacing — so the ORIGINAL trip-wire summary still sits on the page and the QA parser reads the WHOLE body, re-finding the old false tokens (019edfdc UUID, /ready route, bare deletion-v2.ts, '413 tests'). Therefore execute complete can NEVER clear this (each run appends again); only replace_content would strip the stale block, which conflicts with the AGENTS.md hard rule 'never hand-edit Stage / always finalize via complete'. This is an unresolvable-within-mandate deadlock. NO whack-a-mole — builder released. RECOMMEND (operator): force-close WI-825 (audit-only-close already ruled GO; work confirmed correct, real deletion gaps owned by WI-849) AND fix the reviewer harness — it has THREE compounding bugs: (1) Windows-doppler-on-Mac test-runner, (2) brittle evidence-parser misreading UUIDs/routes/bare-filenames as claims, (3) it parses the cumulative appended page body so stale complete-summaries never clear. WI-820 is Closed/Done; refine track done (818/822 Zuzka, 782 parked).

resolved — WS-20 Flow Remediation LANE COMPLETE. WI-825 operator force-closed (Stage=Closed, 13:39:36) per fr-orch-008. Execute-to-close: WI-820 Closed/Done + WI-825 Closed (audit correct+merged 6ec8b38, real deletion gaps owned by WI-849). Refine-to-Ready: WI-818 + WI-822 at Ready (released to Zuzka), WI-782 at Ready (parked, re-home=stay). All 6 mandated items terminal from my side. Tearing down my WI-825 Cosmo Stage monitor; inbox watcher left armed for any closeout word. Reusable learning captured to project_cosmo_shepherd_finalization memory (parser-clean-first-complete + append/cumulative-parse deadlock).

STANDING DOWN per fr-orch-009. WS-20 execute+refine mandate COMPLETE: WI-820 + WI-825 Closed/Done (deletion gaps -> WI-849); WI-818 + WI-822 Ready (Zuzka); WI-782 Ready (parked, re-home=stay). All shepherd monitors (Cosmo Stage + inbox watcher) torn down — shepherd OFFLINE. WS-20 container correctly stays Open (Zuzka-owned 818/822 + parked 782 remain; not my scope). This outbox line is my final entry.

Shepherd BACK ON STATION — operator corrected my premature stand-down (fr-orch-009 was an execute-mandate release, not a lane-graduation; WS-20 stays Open). Re-armed: inbox watcher + Cosmo Stage monitor over the 3 open WS-20 items (WI-818, WI-822, WI-782). Status read: 818/822 show recent edits (Zuzka executing); 782 parked at Ready for the post-Ready operator decision. Holding station to drive WS-20 to Cosmo Close. Confirming exact remaining scope with operator now.

OPERATOR DIRECTED: I take WI-818 all the way to Close (mandate expands from monitor-only to execute). HEADS-UP to avoid double-execution with Zuzka: I am claiming WI-818 and dispatching a Builder to resolve the REAL review-bounce SHOULD_FIX (16:52) — apps/mobile/e2e-web/helpers/mentor-audit-storage-state.ts docblock (L14-17: claims revoked storage keeps the invalid token + exercises the Hono RPC revoked-token-refresh path) DIVERGES from behavior (L155-160 clears Clerk cookies before the revoked banner). NOT a harness misfire — a genuine DoD finding. Fix = reconcile docblock vs coverage to the real revoked-banner flow, surgical main-based PR, parser-clean completion summary (harness lesson), Fixed-In = merge commit (the 13:08/13:12 'not-ancestor-of-main' lesson). Will flag merge-readiness for your merge per the lane pattern. WS-20 now: 820/825/822 Closed; 818 mine-in-progress; 782 parked.

OPERATOR INSTRUCTION (relayed at Jorn's explicit request, so you're aware on resume): the operator has directed THIS shepherd to take WI-818 ALL THE WAY THROUGH to Cosmo Close — my mandate is expanded from monitor-only to EXECUTE for WI-818. Action already taken: claimed WI-818, dispatched a Builder to fix the real DoD SHOULD_FIX (mentor-audit-storage-state.ts docblock/coverage divergence) as a surgical main-based PR. REQUESTING FOLLOW-UP from you: (1) ACK this mandate change + ensure Zuzka stands off WI-818 (no double-execution); (2) coordinate the MERGE of the forthcoming WI-818 fix PR per the lane's orchestrator-merges pattern (Fixed-In must be an ancestor of origin/main BEFORE I finalize — this item already ate 2 not-on-main bounces); (3) keep the fleet-level reviewer-harness fix (prg18fr-012: Windows-doppler-on-Mac runner + parser artifacts + append/cumulative-parse) moving, since WI-818's re-finalize will hit it; (4) track WS-20 to graduation — currently 820/825/822 Closed, 818 mine-in-progress, 782 parked for the operator's post-Ready call. I will flag WI-818 merge-readiness here when the PR is strict-green.

WI-818 PR #1249 is MERGE-READY NOW. Strict-green: claude-review APPROVED — the original SHOULD_FIX (apps/mobile/e2e-web/helpers/mentor-audit-storage-state.ts docblock-vs-behavior divergence) is CLEARED, not re-raised; CodeRabbit clean (1 doc-hygiene warning, addressed in-thread); mergeStateStatus=CLEAN, 0 failed. Comment-only docblock fix, lowest-risk merge. Per prg18fr-017 you were to coordinate the WI-818 merge per the lane's orchestrator-merges pattern — it's ready. QUESTION: do you merge #1249, or delegate the merge to me? (Operator directed me to take WI-818 all the way through, so I can merge + finalize end-to-end if you delegate.) On your call I resume wi818-builder: detach HEAD to the merge commit -> /cosmo:execute complete (parser-clean summary, Fixed In = merge commit) -> Reviewing. Builder standing by.

resolved (fr-orch-010): merge question MOOT — you already merged #1249 (commit 4ac215be, on origin/main, passed the now-required flag-on lane). End-to-end 818 ownership CONFIRMED to me; finalize delegated. Resuming wi818-builder: detach HEAD to 4ac215be -> /cosmo:execute complete, Fixed In = 4ac215be (OVERWRITING the stale page value 16dd03774 from the first landing — builder will verify the overwrite, STOP+report if not), parser-clean summary -> Reviewing. Will flag WS-20 graduation-readiness when 818 Closes (820/825/822 already Closed; 782 parked for operator post-Ready call). Noted: reviewer-harness fix = WI-851 (rel. design WI-866), operator owns prioritization.

WI-818 finalized -> Stage=Reviewing, Fixed In=4ac215be CONFIRMED (the #1249 merge, overwrote stale 16dd0377 + the mis-derived 3aac30ca). NOTE for loop health: /cosmo:execute complete first MIS-DERIVED Fixed In = ambient 3aac30ca because the worktree was DIRTY (uncommitted eas.json env:sync artifact -> HEAD-fallback bug); resolved AGENTS.md-compliantly by git-restoring eas.json -> clean tree -> re-run complete (no hand-edit). WATCH-RISK: the page now carries FOUR cumulative summary blocks (append-only complete x4 across both landings); if the reviewer bounces on cosmo:qa parser/cumulative-body artifacts (the WI-825 deadlock), I will escalate to you for replace_content authorization since complete cannot clear stale blocks. Otherwise the reviewer closes it -> I flag WS-20 graduation-readiness (820/825/822 already Closed; only 782 parked remains after 818).

WI-818 CLOSED by the reviewer (19:37) — the 4-block cumulative-parse risk did NOT materialize; clean close. My execute mandate for WI-818 is COMPLETE end-to-end (fix -> #1249 merged 4ac215be -> finalize -> Reviewing -> Closed). WS-20 GRADUATION-READINESS (you asked me to flag this): 820/825/822/818 all Closed; the ONLY remaining open item is WI-782 (parked at Ready). WS-20 cannot reach Status=Closed until WI-782 is dispositioned, which is the OPERATOR's pending post-Ready call (refine-only mandate; not mine to execute without direction). So: lane is graduation-ready modulo the WI-782 operator decision. Standing by; monitors still armed.
